result.markdown:
--- title: "Sensitivity Analysis: Contentious Effect Sizes" subtitle: "How robust is the combined overall effect to the studies flagged by the cross-dataset validation?" author: "Eduardo S. A. Santos & Collaborators" date: today format: html: toc: true toc-depth: 3 theme: cosmo code-fold: true code-summary: "Show R code" bibliography: ../../references.bib --- ## Why this chapter exists The [Cross-Dataset Extraction Validation](../3_original_dataset_crosscheck/extraction_validation.qmd) chapter compared the two original datasets that feed our combined synthesis — Davies et al. [-@daviesMetaanalysisFactorsInfluencing2020] (continuous, Hedges' *d*) and Jones & DuVal [-@jonesMechanismsSocialInfluence2019] (binary, log odds ratio) — and returned to the source PDFs for the **seven studies whose effect sizes disagreed by more than 0.5 Hedges-*d* units**. That re-extraction reached two conclusions that matter for the headline result: 1. On the **continuous (Davies) side**, the disagreements trace to a handful of identifiable extraction problems (a wrong outcome construct, two `escalc` computational artifacts, a sign error, and one defensible pool-vs-split). Several of these inflate the Davies effect size. 2. On the **binary (Jones) side**, the *point estimates* are sound, but a few rows are over-counted (one spurious value and one duplicated contrast), and the variances of the single-group "vs-chance" studies are inflated by a phantom 50/50 control. A natural question follows: **does the combined overall effect depend on these contentious effect sizes?** This chapter answers it directly. We re-fit the combined multilevel models — using the *same* model structure as the [Overall Effect](overall_effect.qmd) chapter — under three versions of the data and compare the pooled estimates. * **Main** — the data exactly as analysed in the overall-effect chapter (this should reproduce the headline combined estimate). * **Leave-out** — every effect size from the seven contentious studies is removed. This is the conservative bound: it asks what the result would be if we simply did not trust those studies at all. * **Corrected** — the contentious effect sizes are replaced with the design-faithful values re-extracted from the source PDFs. This is the best-estimate version: it keeps the studies but fixes the specific problems the validation identified. ::: {.callout-note} ## Scope The seven contentious studies (Applebaum & Cruz 2000, Briggs et al. 1996, Dugatkin 1992, Dugatkin & Godin 1993, Fowler-Finn et al. 2015, Gierszewski et al. 2018, Howard et al. 1998) are all pre-2019 and were **not** independently re-extracted in our new (Santos) dataset. They therefore enter the combined data **only** through the Davies (continuous) and Jones (binary) datasets, so every edit below acts on legacy rows only — the new extraction is untouched. ::: --- ## Data loading and alignment We rebuild the two combined datasets (`db_hedges`, `db_or`) with the identical loading, species-name reconciliation, and column-alignment logic used in the overall-effect chapter, so the **Main** models here are the same models reported there. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Paths to legacy and new datasets path_new <- here::here("data", "1_effect_size_calculation_pipeline", "Data Extraction Mate Choice Meta Analysis.xlsx") path_davies <- here::here("data", "original_meta_analyses_datasets", "Davies_et_al_2020_Final_data.xlsx") path_jones <- here::here("data", "original_meta_analyses_datasets", "Jones_DuVal_2019_data.CSV") db_new_raw <- readxl::read_excel(path_new, sheet = "EduardoExtraction") db_davies_raw <- readxl::read_excel(path_davies, sheet = "Data") db_jones_raw <- readr::read_csv(path_jones, show_col_types = FALSE) # Species-name reconciliation (matches build_phylogeny.R / overall_effect.qmd) recode_new <- c("Taeniopygia guttata castanotis" = "Taeniopygia guttata") recode_davies <- c("Schizocosa" = "Schizocosa ocreata") jones_binomial <- tibble::tribble( ~family, ~epithet, ~species_phylo, "Adrianichthyidae", "latipes", "Oryzias latipes", "Blenniidae", "nitidus", "Rhabdoblennius nitidus", "Gasterosteidae", "aculeatus", "Gasterosteus aculeatus", "Gobiidae", "minutu", "Pomatoschistus minutus", "Percidae", "olmstedi", "Etheostoma olmstedi", "Poeciliidae", "latipinna", "Poecilia latipinna", "Poeciliidae", "nigrofasciata", "Limia nigrofasciata", "Poeciliidae", "perugiae", "Limia perugiae", "Poeciliidae", "reticulata", "Poecilia reticulata", "Pomacentridae", "leucogaster", "Amblyglyphidodon leucogaster", "Lycosidae", "ocreata", "Schizocosa ocreata", "Estrildidae", "guttata", "Taeniopygia guttata", "Icteridae", "ater", "Molothrus ater", "Muscicapidae", "hypoleuca", "Ficedula hypoleuca", "Drosophilidae", "melanogaster", "Drosophila melanogaster", "Drosophilidae", "serrata", "Drosophila serrata", "Ocypodidae", "mjoebergi", "Uca mjoebergi" ) read_phylo <- function(f) { p <- here::here("outputs", "2_phylogeny", f) if (file.exists(p)) readRDS(p) else NULL } phylo_cor_hedges <- read_phylo("phylo_cor_hedges_model.rds") phylo_cor_or <- read_phylo("phylo_cor_or_model.rds") # New extraction (drop rows flagged for removal) db_new <- db_new_raw %>% filter(is.na(RemoveFromDataset) | RemoveFromDataset != "Yes") %>% mutate( effectSizeCalculatedHedgesD = as.numeric(effectSizeCalculatedHedgesD), effectSizeCalculatedHedgesDVariance = as.numeric(effectSizeCalculatedHedgesDVariance), effectSizeCalculatedOddsRatio = as.numeric(effectSizeCalculatedOddsRatio), effectSizeCalculatedOddsRatioVariance = as.numeric(effectSizeCalculatedOddsRatioVariance), logOddsRatio = log(effectSizeCalculatedOddsRatio), logOddsRatioVariance = effectSizeCalculatedOddsRatioVariance ) # --- Hedges' g (continuous) --------------------------------------------------- hedges_new <- db_new %>% filter(!is.na(effectSizeCalculatedHedgesD), !is.na(effectSizeCalculatedHedgesDVariance)) %>% transmute( yi = effectSizeCalculatedHedgesD, vi = effectSizeCalculatedHedgesDVariance, identifierStudyId = as.character(identifierStudyId), identifierEffectSizeID = as.character(identifierEffectSizeID), species_phylo = dplyr::recode(str_squish(as.character(taxonomySpecies)), !!!recode_new), taxonomySpecies = str_to_lower(word(as.character(taxonomySpecies), -1)), Dataset_Source = "New Extraction (Santos)" ) hedges_davies <- db_davies_raw %>% mutate(Hedges_d_directional = as.numeric(Hedges_d_directional), Variance = as.numeric(Variance)) %>% filter(!is.na(Hedges_d_directional), !is.na(Variance), Variance > 0) %>% transmute( yi = Hedges_d_directional, vi = Variance, identifierStudyId = as.character(Author), identifierEffectSizeID = as.character(Effect_size_no), species_phylo = dplyr::recode(str_squish(as.character(Species_latin)), !!!recode_davies), taxonomySpecies = str_to_lower(word(as.character(Species_latin), -1)), Dataset_Source = "Original (Davies 2020)" ) db_hedges <- bind_rows(hedges_new, hedges_davies) # --- Log odds ratio (binary) -------------------------------------------------- or_new <- db_new %>% filter(!is.na(logOddsRatio), !is.na(logOddsRatioVariance)) %>% transmute( yi = logOddsRatio, vi = logOddsRatioVariance, identifierStudyId = as.character(identifierStudyId), identifierEffectSizeID = as.character(identifierEffectSizeID), species_phylo = dplyr::recode(str_squish(as.character(taxonomySpecies)), !!!recode_new), taxonomySpecies = str_to_lower(word(as.character(taxonomySpecies), -1)), Dataset_Source = "New Extraction (Santos)" ) or_jones <- db_jones_raw %>% filter(!is.na(lnOR), !is.na(VlnOR), VlnOR > 0) %>% mutate(family = str_squish(as.character(Family)), epithet = str_squish(as.character(Species))) %>% left_join(jones_binomial, by = c("family", "epithet")) %>% transmute( yi = lnOR, vi = VlnOR, identifierStudyId = as.character(study), identifierEffectSizeID = as.character(study_Exp), species_phylo = species_phylo, taxonomySpecies = str_to_lower(as.character(Species)), Dataset_Source = "Original (Jones & DuVal 2019)" ) db_or <- bind_rows(or_new, or_jones) ``` ::: --- ## Identifying the contentious effect sizes We read the re-extraction file produced by the validation chapter. Each of its rows is one effect size that we returned to the source PDF for, and it carries three things side by side: the **re-extracted** value (independent, design-faithful), the **Jones** value, and the **Davies** value. From this file alone we can locate every row that needs editing in the two combined datasets — by `identifierEffectSizeID`, never by guesswork. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} reext_path <- here::here("outputs", "3_original_dataset_crosscheck", "reextraction_flagged_7studies.csv") reext <- readr::read_csv(reext_path, show_col_types = FALSE) # Conversion constant: lnOR -> Hedges' d (logistic-to-normal; Borenstein et al. 2009). # Matches the validation chapter; the small-sample (J) correction is ~1 at these # sample sizes and is omitted so the numbers agree with that chapter. LNOR_TO_D <- sqrt(3) / pi # ~ 0.5513 VAR_LNOR_TO_VAR_D <- 3 / pi^2 # = LNOR_TO_D^2 ~ 0.3040 reext_map <- reext %>% transmute( study = identifierStudyId, # Jones effect-size code in the dataset has no slash (e.g. "APP2000THE/1" -> "APP2000THE1") jones_es_id = str_remove(compare_jones_code, "/"), # Davies effect-size number, parsed from e.g. "Davies study 3 (3.1)" -> "3.1" davies_es_id = str_match(compare_davies_es, "\\(([0-9]+\\.[0-9]+)")[, 2], davies_study_no = str_match(compare_davies_es, "study ([0-9]+)")[, 2], species_phylo = str_squish(as.character(taxonomySpecies)), # Re-extracted values reext_lnOR = log(effectSizeCalculatedEstimate), reext_var_lnOR = effectSizeCalculatedVariance, reext_d = log(effectSizeCalculatedEstimate) * LNOR_TO_D, reext_var_d = effectSizeCalculatedVariance * VAR_LNOR_TO_VAR_D, # Prior values (for the audit table) jones_lnOR = compare_jones_lnOR, jones_var = compare_jones_VlnOR, davies_d = compare_davies_hedges_d, davies_var = compare_davies_variance ) # The set of contentious studies, expressed in each dataset's own identifiers. davies_studies_flagged <- sort(unique(as.integer(reext_map$davies_study_no))) # Davies study numbers jones_papers_flagged <- sort(unique(str_extract(reext_map$jones_es_id, "^[A-Za-z]+[0-9]{4}"))) # Jones paper prefixes # Jones rows the validation identified as over-counts to be dropped entirely: # * DUG1992REV1 — spurious third value (reproduces from no experiment pair) # * GIE2018MAT1/2 — duplicate of the GIE2018USI contrast (over-count) jones_drop_overcount <- c("DUG1992REV1", "GIE2018MAT1", "GIE2018MAT2") ``` ::: ### The edit map (auditable) Every change made in the **Corrected** version is listed below. Continuous edits act on Davies rows; binary edits act on Jones rows. Two structural cases are highlighted: Fowler-Finn (Davies pooled one effect; the re-extraction splits it into two arms) and Applebaum (Davies extracted an affiliation-time construct as several effect sizes; only the reversal-frequency effect is retained). ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} |Item |Value | |:------------------------------------------|:-------------------------------------------------------------| |Contentious studies (papers) |7 | |Re-extracted effect sizes |12 | |Davies study numbers affected (continuous) |3, 10, 20, 27, 31, 36, 46 | |Jones papers affected (binary) |APP2000, BRI1996, DUG1992, DUG1993, FOW2015, GIE2018, HOW1998 | |Jones over-count rows dropped (binary) |DUG1992REV1, GIE2018MAT1, GIE2018MAT2 | ::: ::: --- ## Building the three dataset versions ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- # Helper: integer (study) part of a Davies effect-size id ("27.1" -> 27) # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- davies_study_int <- function(es_id) as.integer(str_extract(as.character(es_id), "^[0-9]+")) jones_paper <- function(es_id) str_extract(as.character(es_id), "^[A-Za-z]+[0-9]{4}") # =========================================================================== # CONTINUOUS (Hedges' g) — Davies side # =========================================================================== is_davies <- db_hedges$Dataset_Source == "Original (Davies 2020)" # (1) LEAVE-OUT: drop all Davies rows from the contentious studies. hedges_leaveout <- db_hedges %>% filter(!(Dataset_Source == "Original (Davies 2020)" & davies_study_int(identifierEffectSizeID) %in% davies_studies_flagged)) # (2) CORRECTED: remove all Davies rows from the contentious studies, then add the # re-extracted replacements (one row per re-extracted effect size). For Fowler-Finn # this turns Davies' single pooled effect into two arm-level effects; for Applebaum # it keeps only the reversal-frequency effect (the 3.2-3.4 affiliation-time rows are # not reinstated). identifierStudyId is set to the re-extraction's paper id. hedges_reext_rows <- reext_map %>% transmute( yi = reext_d, vi = reext_var_d, identifierStudyId = study, identifierEffectSizeID = paste0("reext_", jones_es_id), species_phylo = dplyr::recode(species_phylo, !!!recode_davies), taxonomySpecies = str_to_lower(word(species_phylo, -1)), Dataset_Source = "Original (Davies 2020)" ) hedges_corrected <- bind_rows(hedges_leaveout, hedges_reext_rows) # =========================================================================== # BINARY (log OR) — Jones side # =========================================================================== # (1) LEAVE-OUT: drop all Jones rows from the contentious papers. or_leaveout <- db_or %>% filter(!(Dataset_Source == "Original (Jones & DuVal 2019)" & jones_paper(identifierEffectSizeID) %in% jones_papers_flagged)) # (2) CORRECTED: (a) drop the over-count rows; (b) replace lnOR & variance of the # re-extracted codes with the design-faithful values; leave all other Jones rows # (e.g. the legitimate, un-re-extracted APP2000THE2 baseline contrast) untouched. or_replacements <- reext_map %>% select(identifierEffectSizeID = jones_es_id, reext_lnOR, reext_var_lnOR) or_corrected <- db_or %>% filter(!identifierEffectSizeID %in% jones_drop_overcount) %>% left_join(or_replacements, by = "identifierEffectSizeID") %>% mutate( yi = if_else(!is.na(reext_lnOR), reext_lnOR, yi), vi = if_else(!is.na(reext_var_lnOR), reext_var_lnOR, vi) ) %>% select(-reext_lnOR, -reext_var_lnOR) # Quick integrity report (how many rows each edit touched) edit_report <- tibble( Version = c("Hedges' g: Main", "Hedges' g: Leave-out", "Hedges' g: Corrected", "Log OR: Main", "Log OR: Leave-out", "Log OR: Corrected"), `Effect sizes (k)` = c(nrow(db_hedges), nrow(hedges_leaveout), nrow(hedges_corrected), nrow(db_or), nrow(or_leaveout), nrow(or_corrected)) ) ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} Table: Number of effect sizes in each dataset version. |Version | Effect sizes (k)| |:--------------------|----------------:| |Hedges' g: Main | 227| |Hedges' g: Leave-out | 213| |Hedges' g: Corrected | 225| |Log OR: Main | 172| |Log OR: Leave-out | 156| |Log OR: Corrected | 169| ::: ::: --- ## Re-fitting the combined models We use the **same** multilevel model as the overall-effect chapter: `rma.mv` with a variance–covariance matrix (`vcalc`, within-study $\rho = 0.5$), random intercepts for study, effect size, and species, and a phylogenetic random effect supplied with the species correlation matrix (it falls back to the non-phylogenetic structure if a species is not in the matrix). ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} fit_overall_mv <- function(dat, cluster_var = "identifierStudyId", obs_var = "identifierEffectSizeID", rho = 0.5, phylo_cor = NULL, phylo_var = "species_phylo") { dat2 <- dat %>% filter(!is.na(yi), !is.na(vi), vi > 0) if (nrow(dat2) == 0) return(NULL) VCV <- metafor::vcalc(vi = dat2$vi, cluster = dat2[[cluster_var]], obs = dat2[[obs_var]], rho = rho, data = dat2) random_list <- list(~ 1 | identifierStudyId, ~ 1 | identifierEffectSizeID, ~ 1 | taxonomySpecies) R_arg <- NULL if (!is.null(phylo_cor) && phylo_var %in% colnames(dat2)) { sp <- unique(as.character(dat2[[phylo_var]])) if (length(setdiff(sp, rownames(phylo_cor))) == 0) { dat2[[phylo_var]] <- factor(as.character(dat2[[phylo_var]])) Csub <- phylo_cor[sp, sp, drop = FALSE] random_list <- c(random_list, list(stats::as.formula(paste0("~ 1 | ", phylo_var)))) R_arg <- stats::setNames(list(Csub), phylo_var) } } mod <- metafor::rma.mv(yi = dat2$yi, V = VCV, random = random_list, R = R_arg, test = "t", method = "REML", sparse = TRUE, data = dat2) list(data = dat2, model = mod) } # Fit all six models fits <- list( hedges_main = fit_overall_mv(db_hedges, phylo_cor = phylo_cor_hedges), hedges_leaveout = fit_overall_mv(hedges_leaveout, phylo_cor = phylo_cor_hedges), hedges_corrected = fit_overall_mv(hedges_corrected, phylo_cor = phylo_cor_hedges), or_main = fit_overall_mv(db_or, phylo_cor = phylo_cor_or), or_leaveout = fit_overall_mv(or_leaveout, phylo_cor = phylo_cor_or), or_corrected = fit_overall_mv(or_corrected, phylo_cor = phylo_cor_or) ) ``` ::: ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} # Pull a tidy one-row summary from each fitted model summarise_fit <- function(fit, class_lab, version_lab) { if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL) m <- fit$model i2 <- tryCatch(sum(orchaRd::i2_ml(m)["I2_Total"]), error = function(e) NA_real_) tibble( Class = class_lab, Version = version_lab, k = m$k, Studies = length(unique(fit$data$identifierStudyId)), Estimate = as.numeric(m$b[1]), SE = m$se[1], CI_low = m$ci.lb[1], CI_high = m$ci.ub[1], t = m$zval[1], p = m$pval[1], I2 = i2 ) } res <- bind_rows( summarise_fit(fits$hedges_main, "Hedges' g", "Main"), summarise_fit(fits$hedges_leaveout, "Hedges' g", "Leave-out"), summarise_fit(fits$hedges_corrected, "Hedges' g", "Corrected"), summarise_fit(fits$or_main, "Log OR", "Main"), summarise_fit(fits$or_leaveout, "Log OR", "Leave-out"), summarise_fit(fits$or_corrected, "Log OR", "Corrected") ) %>% mutate(Version = factor(Version, levels = c("Main", "Leave-out", "Corrected"))) ``` ::: --- ## Results ### Comparison table For the log odds ratio, the estimate is also shown back-transformed to the odds-ratio scale (`exp`). `k` is the number of effect sizes and `Studies` the number of study clusters in each version. ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output-display} ```{=html}
``` ::: ::: ### Comparison plot ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code} ver_cols <- c("Main" = "#444444", "Leave-out" = "#D55E00", "Corrected" = "#0072B2") make_panel <- function(df, title, xlab) { ggplot(df, aes(Estimate, fct_rev(Version), colour = Version)) + geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", colour = "grey60") + geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = CI_low, xmax = CI_high), height = 0.18, linewidth = 0.9) + geom_point(size = 3.2) + geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%s [%s, %s] (k=%d)", fmt(Estimate, 2), fmt(CI_low, 2), fmt(CI_high, 2), k)), vjust = -1.1, size = 3.1, show.legend = FALSE) + scale_colour_manual(values = ver_cols, guide = "none") + labs(x = xlab, y = NULL, title = title) + theme_minimal(base_size = 12) + theme(plot.title = element_text(face = "bold")) } p_h <- make_panel(filter(res, Class == "Hedges' g"), "Hedges' g (continuous)", "Hedges' g") p_o <- make_panel(filter(res, Class == "Log OR"), "Log odds ratio (binary)", "log OR") p_h + p_o ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![Combined overall effect (point) and 95% confidence interval under the Main, Leave-out, and Corrected datasets. Left: Hedges' g (dashed line at 0). Right: log odds ratio (dashed line at 0, i.e. OR = 1).](sensitivity_analysis_files/figure-html/fig-sensitivity-1.png){#fig-sensitivity width=1056} ::: ::: --- ## Interpretation ::: {.cell} ::: **Continuous (Hedges' *g*).** The combined mean is $0.45$ in the **Main** analysis (statistically significant), $0.42$ when the contentious studies are **left out** (statistically significant), and $0.42$ when they are **corrected** (statistically significant). Because the validation showed the Davies errors mostly *inflate* the effect, the leave-out and corrected means are expected to move toward — not away from — zero relative to Main; the size of that shift is the practical measure of how much the headline continuous estimate leaned on the flagged extractions. **Binary (log odds ratio).** The combined mean is $0.59$ in **Main** (statistically significant), $0.57$ **leaving out** the contentious papers (statistically significant), and $0.58$ when **corrected** (statistically significant). On the binary side the corrections act mainly on *variances* (removing the phantom-control inflation) and remove two over-counted rows, so the point estimate is expected to move less than the continuous one; the main effect there is on precision and weighting rather than on the location of the mean. **Reading the three together.** The **Leave-out** column is the conservative bound (trust nothing flagged); the **Corrected** column is the best estimate (keep the studies, fix the errors). If both fall close to **Main** and keep the same sign and significance, the overall conclusion is robust to the extraction problems the validation uncovered. If **Main** sits apart from the other two, the headline estimate depended materially on the contentious effect sizes, and the **Corrected** value should be preferred as the bias-reduced summary. ::: {.callout-warning} ## What this analysis does and does not show This is a sensitivity analysis of the **combined overall mean** to the seven studies the cross-dataset validation flagged. It does **not** re-audit the rest of the literature, and it inherits every other modelling choice from the overall-effect chapter (the multilevel structure, the within-study $\rho = 0.5$, and the lnOR$\leftrightarrow$*d* scale conversion). The "Corrected" edits are exactly those documented in the [validation chapter](../3_original_dataset_crosscheck/extraction_validation.qmd) and listed in the edit map above; no other values were changed. ::: --- ## Session info ::: callout-note ## R session ::: {.cell} ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` R version 4.6.0 (2026-04-24) Platform: aarch64-apple-darwin23 Running under: macOS Tahoe 26.5.1 Matrix products: default BLAS: /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.6/Resources/lib/libRblas.0.dylib LAPACK: /Library/Frameworks/R.framework/Versions/4.6/Resources/lib/libRlapack.dylib; LAPACK version 3.12.1 locale: [1] en_CA/en_CA/en_CA/C/en_CA/en_CA time zone: America/Edmonton tzcode source: internal attached base packages: [1] stats graphics grDevices utils datasets methods base other attached packages: [1] scales_1.4.0 DT_0.34.0 patchwork_1.3.2 [4] orchaRd_2.2.0 metafor_5.0-1 numDeriv_2016.8-1.1 [7] metadat_1.6-0 Matrix_1.7-5 lubridate_1.9.5 [10] forcats_1.0.1 stringr_1.6.0 dplyr_1.2.1 [13] purrr_1.2.2 readr_2.2.0 tidyr_1.3.2 [16] tibble_3.3.1 ggplot2_4.0.3 tidyverse_2.0.0 [19] readxl_1.5.0 here_1.0.2 loaded via a namespace (and not attached): [1] sass_0.4.10 generics_0.1.4 stringi_1.8.7 lattice_0.22-9 [5] hms_1.1.4 digest_0.6.39 magrittr_2.0.5 evaluate_1.0.5 [9] grid_4.6.0 timechange_0.4.0 RColorBrewer_1.1-3 fastmap_1.2.0 [13] cellranger_1.1.0 rprojroot_2.1.1 jsonlite_2.0.0 crosstalk_1.2.2 [17] jquerylib_0.1.4 cli_3.6.6 crayon_1.5.3 rlang_1.2.0 [21] bit64_4.8.2 cachem_1.1.0 withr_3.0.2 yaml_2.3.12 [25] otel_0.2.0 parallel_4.6.0 tools_4.6.0 tzdb_0.5.0 [29] mathjaxr_2.0-0 pacman_0.5.1 vctrs_0.7.3 R6_2.6.1 [33] lifecycle_1.0.5 bit_4.6.0 htmlwidgets_1.6.4 vroom_1.7.1 [37] pkgconfig_2.0.3 bslib_0.11.0 pillar_1.11.1 gtable_0.3.6 [41] glue_1.8.1 xfun_0.58 tidyselect_1.2.1 knitr_1.51 [45] farver_2.1.2 nlme_3.1-169 htmltools_0.5.9 labeling_0.4.3 [49] rmarkdown_2.31 compiler_4.6.0 S7_0.2.2 ``` ::: ::: :::
